front: What is the definition of a closed set in a metric space (X, d)?
back: A subset F of a metric space (X, d) is closed if its complement, X \ F, is an open set.

front: How is a closed set related to its limit points?
back: A set F is closed if and only if it contains all of its limit points. That is, F' ⊆ F, where F' is the derived set (set of all limit points).

front: What is the closure of a set A, denoted Ā or cl(A)?
back: The closure of A is the smallest closed set containing A. It is the union of A and its set of limit points, A' (i.e., Ā = A ∪ A').

front: State an important property regarding the intersection of closed sets.
back: The intersection of any collection (finite or infinite) of closed sets is itself a closed set.

front: State an important property regarding the union of closed sets.
back: The union of a finite number of closed sets is a closed set. (Note: The union of infinitely many closed sets may not be closed.)
